/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba src/convergence_attestor.py | sed -n '430,950p'" in /Users/Zhuanz/Desktop/cfd-harness-unified
 succeeded in 0ms:
   430	    r"\s*Final residual\s*=\s*([\deE+.\-]+),\s*No Iterations\s+(\d+)"
   431	)
   432	
   433	_BOUNDING_LINE_RE = re.compile(r"^\s*bounding\s+(k|epsilon|omega|nuTilda|nut)\b")
   434	# OpenFOAM writes `Time = 123` on its own line AND as `Time = 123s` with
   435	# trailing `s`. Accept either form; trailing whitespace tolerated.
   436	_TIME_STEP_RE = re.compile(r"^Time\s*=\s*[\deE+.\-]+s?\s*$")
   437	_A1_FATAL_MARKER_RE = re.compile(
   438	    r"FOAM FATAL IO ERROR|FOAM FATAL ERROR|^Floating point exception\b|Floating exception\b"
   439	)
   440	
   441	
   442	def _parse_residual_timeline(log_path: Path) -> dict[str, list[float]]:
   443	    """Extract per-field Initial residual history across all iterations.
   444	
   445	    Returns {"Ux": [...], "Uy": [...], "p": [...], "k": [...], "epsilon": [...]}.
   446	    Order preserves the log's iteration order. Used by A3.
   447	    """
   448	    timeline: dict[str, list[float]] = {}
   449	    with log_path.open("r", encoding="utf-8", errors="replace") as fh:
   450	        for line in fh:
   451	            m = _INITIAL_RESIDUAL_RE.search(line)
   452	            if not m:
   453	                continue
   454	            field_name = m.group(1)
   455	            try:
   456	                r0 = float(m.group(2))
   457	            except ValueError:
   458	                continue
   459	            timeline.setdefault(field_name, []).append(r0)
   460	    return timeline
   461	
   462	
   463	def _parse_outer_iteration_residuals(log_path: Path) -> dict[str, list[float]]:
   464	    """Extract per-field residuals from the first solve seen in each Time block.
   465	
   466	    A6 should reason about outer-iteration progress, not every inner corrector
   467	    solve. For each ``Time = ...`` block, only the first
   468	    ``Solving for <field>, Initial residual = ...`` line per field is kept.
   469	    Subsequent solves of the same field inside that block are treated as
   470	    inner-loop corrections and ignored for the A6 plateau detector.
   471	    """
   472	    return {
   473	        field_name: [residual for residual, _ in samples]
   474	        for field_name, samples in _parse_outer_iteration_samples(log_path).items()
   475	    }
   476	
   477	
   478	def _parse_outer_iteration_samples(log_path: Path) -> dict[str, list[tuple[float, int]]]:
   479	    """Extract ``(initial_residual, iterations)`` for the first solve per field.
   480	
   481	    A6 uses the same outer-iteration grouping as `_parse_outer_iteration_residuals`,
   482	    but keeps the solver's reported iteration count so it can ignore
   483	    non-informative single-iteration algebraic updates and pressure fields that
   484	    are already covered by A4's iteration-cap detector.
   485	    """
   486	    timeline: dict[str, list[tuple[float, int]]] = {}
   487	    seen_fields_in_block: set[str] = set()
   488	    in_time_block = False
   489	    with log_path.open("r", encoding="utf-8", errors="replace") as fh:
   490	        for line in fh:
   491	            if _TIME_STEP_RE.match(line):
   492	                seen_fields_in_block.clear()
   493	                in_time_block = True
   494	                continue
   495	            if not in_time_block:
   496	                continue
   497	            m = _INITIAL_RESIDUAL_RE.search(line)
   498	            if not m:
   499	                continue
   500	            field_name = m.group(1)
   501	            if field_name in seen_fields_in_block:
   502	                continue
   503	            try:
   504	                r0 = float(m.group(2))
   505	                iterations = int(m.group(4))
   506	            except ValueError:
   507	                continue
   508	            timeline.setdefault(field_name, []).append((r0, iterations))
   509	            seen_fields_in_block.add(field_name)
   510	    return timeline
   511	
   512	
   513	def _parse_iteration_caps_per_block(log_path: Path) -> list[int]:
   514	    """Return per-`Time = ...` block the MAX pressure-solver iteration count
   515	    seen inside that block.
   516	
   517	    Codex DEC-038 round-1 BLOCKER 2: A4 must count consecutive TIME STEPS
   518	    (outer iterations), not consecutive solve lines — PIMPLE multi-corrector
   519	    loops emit ≥2 pressure solves per block and the prior line-based count
   520	    would false-fire after 1.5 blocks. Returns one entry per block; a
   521	    block's count is the worst (max) pressure iteration count seen in it.
   522	    Blocks with no pressure solve get 0 so A4 can explicitly reset the
   523	    consecutiveness streak on gaps.
   524	    """
   525	    per_block_max: list[int] = []
   526	    current_max = 0
   527	    seen_any = False
   528	    with log_path.open("r", encoding="utf-8", errors="replace") as fh:
   529	        for line in fh:
   530	            if _TIME_STEP_RE.match(line):
   531	                if seen_any:
   532	                    per_block_max.append(current_max)
   533	                current_max = 0
   534	                seen_any = True
   535	                continue
   536	            m = A4_PRESSURE_FIELD_RE.search(line)
   537	            if m:
   538	                try:
   539	                    count = int(m.group(2))
   540	                except ValueError:
   541	                    continue
   542	                if count > current_max:
   543	                    current_max = count
   544	        if seen_any:
   545	            per_block_max.append(current_max)
   546	    return per_block_max
   547	
   548	
   549	def _parse_bounding_lines_per_step(log_path: Path) -> list[set[str]]:
   550	    """Return list of sets, one per `Time =` block, containing fields that
   551	    bounded in that block. Used by A5.
   552	    """
   553	    blocks: list[set[str]] = [set()]
   554	    with log_path.open("r", encoding="utf-8", errors="replace") as fh:
   555	        for line in fh:
   556	            if _TIME_STEP_RE.match(line):
   557	                blocks.append(set())
   558	                continue
   559	            m = _BOUNDING_LINE_RE.match(line)
   560	            if m:
   561	                blocks[-1].add(m.group(1))
   562	    # Drop leading empty block before first `Time =`.
   563	    if blocks and not blocks[0]:
   564	        blocks.pop(0)
   565	    return blocks
   566	
   567	
   568	def _scan_a1_fatal_lines(log_path: Path) -> list[str]:
   569	    if not log_path.is_file():
   570	        return []
   571	
   572	    fatal_lines: list[str] = []
   573	    with log_path.open("r", encoding="utf-8", errors="replace") as fh:
   574	        for line in fh:
   575	            normalized = line.strip()
   576	            if not normalized:
   577	                continue
   578	            lower = normalized.lower()
   579	            if "floating point exception trapping" in lower:
   580	                continue
   581	            if _A1_FATAL_MARKER_RE.search(normalized):
   582	                fatal_lines.append(normalized[:240])
   583	                if len(fatal_lines) >= 5:
   584	                    break
   585	    return fatal_lines
   586	
   587	
   588	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   589	# Individual checks
   590	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   591	
   592	def _check_a1_solver_crash(log_path: Path, execution_result: Any = None) -> AttestorCheck:
   593	    stats = parse_solver_log(log_path)
   594	    fatal_lines: list[str] = []
   595	    for line in stats.fatal_lines + _scan_a1_fatal_lines(log_path):
   596	        if line and line not in fatal_lines:
   597	            fatal_lines.append(line)
   598	    exec_failed = getattr(execution_result, "success", None) is False
   599	    exit_code = getattr(execution_result, "exit_code", None) if execution_result is not None else None
   600	
   601	    if exec_failed or fatal_lines or stats.fatal_detected:
   602	        evidence: dict[str, Any] = {"fatal_lines": fatal_lines[:3]}
   603	        summary_parts: list[str] = []
   604	        detail_parts: list[str] = []
   605	        if exec_failed:
   606	            evidence["execution_success"] = False
   607	            evidence["exit_code"] = exit_code
   608	            summary_parts.append(
   609	                "execution_result.success=False"
   610	                if exit_code is None
   611	                else f"execution_result.success=False (exit_code={exit_code})"
   612	            )
   613	            detail_parts.append(
   614	                "execution_result reported solver failure"
   615	                if exit_code is None
   616	                else f"execution_result reported solver failure with exit_code={exit_code}"
   617	            )
   618	        if fatal_lines or stats.fatal_detected:
   619	            summary_parts.append(
   620	                fatal_lines[0][:120] if fatal_lines else "fatal marker found in solver log"
   621	            )
   622	            detail_parts.append(
   623	                "solver log contains a FOAM FATAL / floating exception marker"
   624	            )
   625	        return AttestorCheck(
   626	            check_id="A1",
   627	            concern_type="SOLVER_CRASH_LOG",
   628	            verdict="FAIL",
   629	            summary="; ".join(summary_parts)[:240],
   630	            detail=(
   631	                "DEC-V61-038 A1: "
   632	                + "; ".join(detail_parts)
   633	                + ". A1 fails if either the execution_result reports a non-zero-style "
   634	                "failure or the log itself contains fatal markers, because either signal "
   635	                "means the run cannot be trusted."
   636	            )[:2000],
   637	            evidence=evidence,
   638	        )
   639	    return AttestorCheck(
   640	        check_id="A1",
   641	        concern_type="SOLVER_CRASH_LOG",
   642	        verdict="PASS",
   643	        summary="no execution-result failure or fatal marker in log",
   644	        detail="",
   645	    )
   646	
   647	
   648	def _check_a2_continuity_floor(log_path: Path, thresholds: Thresholds) -> AttestorCheck:
   649	    stats = parse_solver_log(log_path)
   650	    sl = stats.final_continuity_sum_local
   651	    if sl is None:
   652	        return AttestorCheck(
   653	            check_id="A2", concern_type="CONTINUITY_NOT_CONVERGED", verdict="PASS",
   654	            summary="no continuity line in log (case may not report it)",
   655	            detail="",
   656	        )
   657	    if sl > thresholds.continuity_floor:
   658	        # Codex DEC-038 round-1 A2/G5 split-brain comment: A2 stays strictly
   659	        # HAZARD here to avoid conflict with G5, which hard-FAILs
   660	        # `sum_local > 1e-2` on the gate side. Keeping A2 as HAZARD means
   661	        # the attestor tier is purely diagnostic; the FAIL call belongs to
   662	        # the gate layer. Previously A2 returned FAIL for >1e-2, but the
   663	        # verdict engine did not hard-FAIL on CONTINUITY_NOT_CONVERGED, so
   664	        # the semantics split across layers. Now A2 is always HAZARD-tier.
   665	        verdict: CheckVerdict = "HAZARD"
   666	        return AttestorCheck(
   667	            check_id="A2",
   668	            concern_type="CONTINUITY_NOT_CONVERGED",
   669	            verdict=verdict,
   670	            summary=(f"final sum_local={sl:.3g} > floor {thresholds.continuity_floor:.0e}")[:240],
   671	            detail=(
   672	                f"DEC-V61-038 A2: incompressible steady continuity error at "
   673	                f"convergence should be ≤ {thresholds.continuity_floor:.0e}. Observed "
   674	                f"final sum_local={sl:.6g}. Values between {thresholds.continuity_floor:.0e} "
   675	                f"and 1e-2 are HAZARD (marginal convergence); >1e-2 is FAIL "
   676	                "(DEC-036b G5 also fires)."
   677	            )[:2000],
   678	            evidence={"sum_local": sl, "threshold": thresholds.continuity_floor},
   679	        )
   680	    return AttestorCheck(
   681	        check_id="A2", concern_type="CONTINUITY_NOT_CONVERGED", verdict="PASS",
   682	        summary=f"final sum_local={sl:.3g} ≤ {thresholds.continuity_floor:.0e}",
   683	        detail="",
   684	    )
   685	
   686	
   687	def _check_a3_residual_floor(log_path: Path, thresholds: Thresholds) -> AttestorCheck:
   688	    timeline = _parse_residual_timeline(log_path)
   689	    if not timeline:
   690	        return AttestorCheck(
   691	            check_id="A3", concern_type="RESIDUALS_ABOVE_TARGET", verdict="PASS",
   692	            summary="no residual lines parsed from log",
   693	            detail="",
   694	        )
   695	    offenders: dict[str, float] = {}
   696	    offender_thresholds: dict[str, float] = {}
   697	    for field_name, history in timeline.items():
   698	        last = history[-1]
   699	        threshold = thresholds.residual_floor_per_field.get(
   700	            field_name,
   701	            thresholds.residual_floor,
   702	        )
   703	        if last > threshold:
   704	            offenders[field_name] = last
   705	            offender_thresholds[field_name] = threshold
   706	    if offenders:
   707	        sorted_off = sorted(offenders.items(), key=lambda kv: -kv[1])
   708	        summary = (
   709	            "final residuals above field targets: "
   710	            + ", ".join(
   711	                f"{field_name}={value:.3g}>{offender_thresholds[field_name]:.3g}"
   712	                for field_name, value in sorted_off[:3]
   713	            )
   714	        )[:240]
   715	        return AttestorCheck(
   716	            check_id="A3", concern_type="RESIDUALS_ABOVE_TARGET",
   717	            verdict="HAZARD",
   718	            summary=summary,
   719	            detail=(
   720	                "DEC-V61-038 A3: at convergence, SIMPLE/PISO initial residuals "
   721	                "should be ≤ each field's configured threshold. Fields listed "
   722	                "above have final-iteration Initial residuals exceeding their "
   723	                "per-field targets. This "
   724	                "may be physically expected for some cases (impinging_jet "
   725	                "p_rgh, RBC oscillatory modes) — HAZARD not FAIL until a "
   726	                "per-case override promotes it."
   727	            )[:2000],
   728	            evidence={
   729	                "offenders": offenders,
   730	                "thresholds_by_field": offender_thresholds,
   731	                "default_threshold": thresholds.residual_floor,
   732	            },
   733	        )
   734	    return AttestorCheck(
   735	        check_id="A3", concern_type="RESIDUALS_ABOVE_TARGET", verdict="PASS",
   736	        summary="all residuals ≤ their configured field thresholds",
   737	        detail="",
   738	    )
   739	
   740	
   741	def _check_a4_iteration_cap(log_path: Path, thresholds: Thresholds) -> AttestorCheck:
   742	    per_block = _parse_iteration_caps_per_block(log_path)
   743	    if not per_block:
   744	        return AttestorCheck(
   745	            check_id="A4", concern_type="SOLVER_ITERATION_CAP", verdict="PASS",
   746	            summary="no pressure solver iteration counts in log",
   747	            detail="",
   748	        )
   749	    consecutive = 0
   750	    max_consecutive = 0
   751	    cap_hits = 0
   752	    max_iterations = 0
   753	    for b_max in per_block:
   754	        if b_max > max_iterations:
   755	            max_iterations = b_max
   756	        if b_max in A4_ITERATION_CAP_VALUES or b_max >= 1000:
   757	            consecutive += 1
   758	            cap_hits += 1
   759	            if consecutive > max_consecutive:
   760	                max_consecutive = consecutive
   761	            if consecutive >= thresholds.iteration_cap_detector_count:
   762	                return AttestorCheck(
   763	                    check_id="A4", concern_type="SOLVER_ITERATION_CAP",
   764	                    verdict="FAIL",
   765	                    summary=(
   766	                        f"pressure solver hit {b_max} iterations in "
   767	                        f"≥ {thresholds.iteration_cap_detector_count} consecutive time-step blocks"
   768	                    )[:240],
   769	                    detail=(
   770	                        "DEC-V61-038 A4: pressure-velocity solver loop is "
   771	                        f"hitting its iteration cap (~{b_max}) in at least "
   772	                        f"{thresholds.iteration_cap_detector_count} consecutive time-step blocks "
   773	                        "(Time = ... dividers). SIMPLE/PISO/PIMPLE coupling "
   774	                        "has effectively failed — the solver is burning CPU "
   775	                        "without reducing the residual. Hard FAIL."
   776	                    )[:2000],
   777	                    evidence={
   778	                        "consecutive_cap_blocks": consecutive,
   779	                        "max_consecutive_cap_blocks": max_consecutive,
   780	                        "final_cap_value": b_max,
   781	                        "total_blocks": len(per_block),
   782	                    },
   783	                )
   784	        else:
   785	            consecutive = 0
   786	    return AttestorCheck(
   787	        check_id="A4", concern_type="SOLVER_ITERATION_CAP", verdict="PASS",
   788	        summary=(
   789	            f"pressure solver peaked at {max_iterations} iterations; "
   790	            f"max consecutive cap streak={max_consecutive}"
   791	            if cap_hits
   792	            else f"no capped pressure-solver blocks across {len(per_block)} time steps"
   793	        )[:240],
   794	        detail="",
   795	    )
   796	
   797	
   798	def _check_a5_bounding_recurrence(log_path: Path, thresholds: Thresholds) -> AttestorCheck:
   799	    blocks = _parse_bounding_lines_per_step(log_path)
   800	    if len(blocks) < 5:
   801	        # Too few time steps to judge recurrence.
   802	        return AttestorCheck(
   803	            check_id="A5", concern_type="BOUNDING_RECURRENT", verdict="PASS",
   804	            summary=f"only {len(blocks)} time-step blocks parsed",
   805	            detail="",
   806	        )
   807	    window = blocks[-thresholds.bounding_recurrence_window:]
   808	    if not window:
   809	        return AttestorCheck(
   810	            check_id="A5", concern_type="BOUNDING_RECURRENT", verdict="PASS",
   811	            summary="no final-window blocks",
   812	            detail="",
   813	        )
   814	    per_field_frac: dict[str, float] = {}
   815	    for field_name in ("k", "epsilon", "omega", "nuTilda", "nut"):
   816	        bounded_count = sum(1 for b in window if field_name in b)
   817	        if bounded_count == 0:
   818	            continue
   819	        frac = bounded_count / len(window)
   820	        per_field_frac[field_name] = frac
   821	    offenders = {k: v for k, v in per_field_frac.items()
   822	                 if v >= thresholds.bounding_recurrence_frac_threshold}
   823	    if offenders:
   824	        top = max(offenders.items(), key=lambda kv: kv[1])
   825	        return AttestorCheck(
   826	            check_id="A5", concern_type="BOUNDING_RECURRENT",
   827	            verdict="HAZARD",
   828	            summary=(
   829	                f"{top[0]} bounded in {top[1]*100:.0f}% of last "
   830	                f"{len(window)} iterations (threshold "
   831	                f"{thresholds.bounding_recurrence_frac_threshold*100:.0f}%)"
   832	            )[:240],
   833	            detail=(
   834	                "DEC-V61-038 A5: turbulence field is being clipped in a large "
   835	                f"fraction of the FINAL {len(window)} iterations. Healthy "
   836	                "convergence shows bounding events in early transients then "
   837	                "stabilises. Recurrent bounding in the tail indicates the "
   838	                "solution never settles — 'converged' residuals are an artefact "
   839	                "of clipping, not physical equilibrium."
   840	            )[:2000],
   841	            evidence={"per_field_fraction": per_field_frac, "window": len(window)},
   842	        )
   843	    return AttestorCheck(
   844	        check_id="A5", concern_type="BOUNDING_RECURRENT", verdict="PASS",
   845	        summary=(
   846	            f"bounding fractions in last {len(window)} iters: "
   847	            + ", ".join(f"{k}={v:.0%}" for k, v in per_field_frac.items())
   848	            if per_field_frac else f"no bounding in last {len(window)} iters"
   849	        )[:240],
   850	        detail="",
   851	    )
   852	
   853	
   854	def _check_a6_no_progress(log_path: Path, thresholds: Thresholds) -> AttestorCheck:
   855	    sample_timeline = _parse_outer_iteration_samples(log_path)
   856	    timeline = _parse_outer_iteration_residuals(log_path)
   857	    if not timeline:
   858	        return AttestorCheck(
   859	            check_id="A6", concern_type="NO_RESIDUAL_PROGRESS", verdict="PASS",
   860	            summary="no outer-iteration residuals parsed",
   861	            detail="",
   862	        )
   863	    offenders: dict[str, dict[str, float]] = {}
   864	    for field_name, history in timeline.items():
   865	        sample_window = sample_timeline.get(field_name, [])[-thresholds.no_progress_window:]
   866	        if len(sample_window) < 2:
   867	            continue
   868	        iterations_window = [iterations for _, iterations in sample_window]
   869	        if all(iterations <= 1 for iterations in iterations_window):
   870	            continue
   871	        if field_name in {"p", "p_rgh", "pd"} and all(
   872	            iterations in A4_ITERATION_CAP_VALUES for iterations in iterations_window
   873	        ):
   874	            continue
   875	        window = history[-thresholds.no_progress_window:]
   876	        lo = min(window)
   877	        hi = max(window)
   878	        if lo <= 0 or hi <= 0:
   879	            continue
   880	        # A6 only fires when residuals are STUCK AT A HIGH PLATEAU — i.e.,
   881	        # still above the A3 floor. If residuals have already decayed to
   882	        # convergence (< 1e-3), a small decade-range in the tail is just
   883	        # machine-noise fluctuation, not "stuck". Guard against this
   884	        # false positive (caught on LDC: Ux plateaued at 1e-5 with 0.02
   885	        # decades range — that's converged, not stuck).
   886	        threshold = thresholds.residual_floor_per_field.get(
   887	            field_name,
   888	            thresholds.residual_floor,
   889	        )
   890	        if hi < threshold:
   891	            continue
   892	        decades = math_log10(hi / lo) if hi > lo else 0.0
   893	        if decades <= thresholds.no_progress_decade_frac:
   894	            offenders[field_name] = {
   895	                "decades": decades,
   896	                "lo": lo,
   897	                "hi": hi,
   898	                "threshold": threshold,
   899	                "window": len(window),
   900	            }
   901	    if offenders:
   902	        worst = min(offenders.items(), key=lambda kv: kv[1]["decades"])
   903	        return AttestorCheck(
   904	            check_id="A6", concern_type="NO_RESIDUAL_PROGRESS",
   905	            verdict="HAZARD",
   906	            summary=(
   907	                f"{worst[0]} residual range over last "
   908	                f"{worst[1]['window']} outer iterations: "
   909	                f"{worst[1]['lo']:.2e} – {worst[1]['hi']:.2e} "
   910	                f"({worst[1]['decades']:.2f} decades)"
   911	            )[:240],
   912	            detail=(
   913	                "DEC-V61-038 A6: first-solve residuals for the fields listed "
   914	                f"above did not decay > {thresholds.no_progress_decade_frac:.1f} decade(s) "
   915	                "over the recent outer-iteration window. Solver is "
   916	                "stuck at a plateau; any scalar extracted from this 'converged' "
   917	                "state is physically ambiguous."
   918	            )[:2000],
   919	            evidence={
   920	                "offenders": offenders,
   921	                "window": worst[1]["window"],
   922	                "requested_window": thresholds.no_progress_window,
   923	            },
   924	        )
   925	    return AttestorCheck(
   926	        check_id="A6", concern_type="NO_RESIDUAL_PROGRESS", verdict="PASS",
   927	        summary=(
   928	            f"all eligible outer-iteration residual histories show > {thresholds.no_progress_decade_frac:.1f} "
   929	            "decade decay in tail window"
   930	        ),
   931	        detail="",
   932	    )
   933	
   934	
   935	def math_log10(x: float) -> float:
   936	    """log10 with a zero-guard. Inlined to avoid a dependency in this module."""
   937	    import math
   938	    if x <= 0:
   939	        return 0.0
   940	    return math.log10(x)
   941	
   942	
   943	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   944	# Public entry point
   945	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   946	
   947	def attest(
   948	    log_path: Optional[Path],
   949	    execution_result: Any = None,
   950	    case_id: Optional[str] = None,

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba ui/backend/services/validation_report.py | sed -n '560,650p'" in /Users/Zhuanz/Desktop/cfd-harness-unified
 succeeded in 0ms:
   560	        return ("UNKNOWN", None, None, lower, upper)
   561	
   562	    # DEC-V61-036 G1 + DEC-V61-036b G3/G4/G5 + DEC-V61-038 A1/A4:
   563	    # hard-FAIL concern codes. When any of these concerns are present,
   564	    # the measurement cannot be trusted regardless of whether it lies
   565	    # inside the gold tolerance band.
   566	    #   G1  MISSING_TARGET_QUANTITY    — schema mismatch (extractor missed gold quantity)
   567	    #   G3  VELOCITY_OVERFLOW           — |U|_max > 100·U_ref
   568	    #   G4  TURBULENCE_NEGATIVE         — k/eps/omega < 0 at last iter or overflow
   569	    #   G5  CONTINUITY_DIVERGED         — sum_local > 1e-2 or |cum| > 1
   570	    #   A1  SOLVER_CRASH_LOG            — FOAM FATAL / stack-trace in log
   571	    #   A4  SOLVER_ITERATION_CAP        — pressure loop hit cap ≥3 consecutive iters
   572	    # A2/A3/A5/A6 are HAZARD tier — the scalar may still be computable, but
   573	    # the convergence state is suspect enough that "within band" is not a
   574	    # meaningful PASS. Per-case thresholds can escalate selected concern
   575	    # codes to FAIL via `promote_to_fail`.
   576	    _HARD_FAIL_CONCERNS = {
   577	        "MISSING_TARGET_QUANTITY",
   578	        "VELOCITY_OVERFLOW",
   579	        "TURBULENCE_NEGATIVE",
   580	        "CONTINUITY_DIVERGED",
   581	        "SOLVER_CRASH_LOG",
   582	        "SOLVER_ITERATION_CAP",
   583	    }
   584	    _HAZARD_TIER_CONCERNS = {
   585	        "CONTINUITY_NOT_CONVERGED",
   586	        "RESIDUALS_ABOVE_TARGET",
   587	        "BOUNDING_RECURRENT",
   588	        "NO_RESIDUAL_PROGRESS",
   589	    }
   590	    has_hard_fail = any(
   591	        c.concern_type in _HARD_FAIL_CONCERNS for c in audit_concerns
   592	    )
   593	    has_hazard_tier = any(
   594	        c.concern_type in _HAZARD_TIER_CONCERNS for c in audit_concerns
   595	    )
   596	    if measurement.value is None or has_hard_fail:
   597	        # Codex DEC-036b round-1 feedback: when a hard-fail concern fires,
   598	        # the scalar measurement cannot be trusted even if it happens to lie
   599	        # in the tolerance band. Returning `within_tolerance=True` under a
   600	        # FAIL verdict rendered as "Within band: yes" while status was FAIL,
   601	        # which is materially confusing. Null the `within` flag whenever
   602	        # the verdict is hard-failed — the UI now renders "—" in that column.
   603	        if measurement.value is None:
   604	            return ("FAIL", None, None, lower, upper)
   605	        dev_pct = 0.0
   606	        if gs_ref.ref_value != 0.0:
   607	            dev_pct = (measurement.value - gs_ref.ref_value) / gs_ref.ref_value * 100.0
   608	        return ("FAIL", dev_pct, None, lower, upper)
   609	
   610	    deviation_pct = 0.0
   611	    if gs_ref.ref_value != 0.0:
   612	        deviation_pct = (measurement.value - gs_ref.ref_value) / gs_ref.ref_value * 100.0
   613	
   614	    if has_hazard_tier:
   615	        if thresholds is not None and thresholds.promote_to_fail:
   616	            promoted_concerns = [
   617	                concern
   618	                for concern in audit_concerns
   619	                if concern.concern_type in thresholds.promote_to_fail
   620	                and concern.concern_type in _HAZARD_TIER_CONCERNS
   621	            ]
   622	            if promoted_concerns:
   623	                return ("FAIL", deviation_pct, None, lower, upper)
   624	        return ("HAZARD", deviation_pct, None, lower, upper)
   625	
   626	    # Tolerance test in deviation space (sign-invariant + consistent with
   627	    # the percentage shown in the UI). `within_tolerance` matches when
   628	    # |deviation| <= tolerance_pct expressed as a percentage.
   629	    within = abs(deviation_pct) <= gs_ref.tolerance_pct * 100.0
   630	    precondition_fails = any(not p.satisfied for p in preconditions)
   631	    has_silent_pass_hazard = any(
   632	        "SILENT_PASS_HAZARD" in c.concern_type or "SILENT_PASS_HAZARD" in (c.summary or "")
   633	        or "SILENT_PASS_HAZARD" in (c.detail or "")
   634	        for c in audit_concerns
   635	    )
   636	
   637	    if not within:
   638	        return ("FAIL", deviation_pct, within, lower, upper)
   639	    if precondition_fails or has_silent_pass_hazard:
   640	        return ("HAZARD", deviation_pct, within, lower, upper)
   641	    return ("PASS", deviation_pct, within, lower, upper)
   642	
   643

   645	    doc: dict[str, Any] | None,

   647	    """DEC-V61-040: lift `attestation` block from the fixture into the API.
   648	
   649	    The attestor runs at audit-fixture time (see scripts/phase5_audit_run.py)
   650	    and writes `{overall, checks[]}` onto the measurement doc. Two states:

   124	        if magnitude is not None:

   128	
   129	def _flow_type_name(task_spec: object | None, case_id: str) -> str:
   130	    flow_type = getattr(task_spec, "flow_type", None)
   131	    flow_value = getattr(flow_type, "value", flow_type)
   132	    if isinstance(flow_value, str) and flow_value:
   133	        return flow_value.upper()
   134	
   135	    cid = case_id.lower()
   136	    if any(token in cid for token in ("differential_heated", "rayleigh_benard")):
   137	        return "NATURAL_CONVECTION"
   138	    if any(token in cid for token in ("airfoil", "cylinder", "impinging")):
   139	        return "EXTERNAL"
   140	    return "INTERNAL"
   141	
   142	
   143	def _resolve_u_ref(task_spec: object | None, case_id: str) -> tuple[float, bool]:
   144	    """Best-effort `U_ref` resolution for DEC-V61-036b G3.
   145	
   146	    Priority order:
   147	      1. Explicit velocity-like boundary-condition keys already present on
   148	         the TaskSpec (e.g. U_ref/U_inf/U_bulk/lid velocity).
   149	      2. Flow-type heuristics / buoyancy-derived estimate when enough
   150	         metadata is present.
   151	      3. Whitelist case registry for known cases not yet plumbed.
   152	      4. Safe fallback `(1.0, False)` with a WARN concern stamped into the
   153	         fixture by `_audit_fixture_doc`.
   154	    """
   155	    boundary_conditions = getattr(task_spec, "boundary_conditions", None) or {}
   156	    if not isinstance(boundary_conditions, dict):
   157	        boundary_conditions = {}
   158	
   159	    direct = _resolve_from_bc_keys(
   160	        boundary_conditions,
   161	        "U_ref",
   162	        "u_ref",
   163	        "U_inf",
   164	        "u_inf",
   165	        "free_stream_velocity",
   166	        "freestream_velocity",
   167	        "inlet_velocity",
   168	        "inlet_u",
   169	        "U_bulk",
   170	        "u_bulk",
   171	        "lid_velocity_u",
   172	        "top_wall_u",
   173	    )
   174	    if direct is not None:
   175	        return direct, True
   176	
   177	    flow_type = _flow_type_name(task_spec, case_id)
   178	    if flow_type == "INTERNAL":
   179	        internal = _resolve_from_bc_keys(
   180	            boundary_conditions,
   181	            "pipe_inlet_velocity",
   182	            "channel_inlet_velocity",
   183	            "duct_inlet_velocity",
   184	            "lid_velocity",
   185	        )
   186	        if internal is not None:
   187	            return internal, True
   188	    elif flow_type == "EXTERNAL":
   189	        external = _resolve_from_bc_keys(
   190	            boundary_conditions,
   191	            "free_stream_u",
   192	            "freestream_u",
   193	            "U_external",
   194	        )
   195	        if external is not None:
   196	            return external, True
   197	    elif flow_type == "NATURAL_CONVECTION":
   198	        g_mag = _resolve_from_bc_keys(boundary_conditions, "g", "gravity", "g_mag")
   199	        beta = _resolve_from_bc_keys(boundary_conditions, "beta")
   200	        delta_t = _resolve_from_bc_keys(boundary_conditions, "dT", "delta_T")
   201	        length_scale = _resolve_from_bc_keys(boundary_conditions, "L", "length_scale")
   202	        if (
   203	            g_mag is not None
   204	            and beta is not None
   205	            and delta_t is not None
   206	            and length_scale is not None
   207	        ):
   208	            return math.sqrt(abs(g_mag * beta * delta_t * length_scale)), True
   209	
   210	    registry_value = _CASE_U_REF_REGISTRY.get(case_id.lower())
   211	    if registry_value is not None:
   212	        return registry_value, True
   213	
   214	    return 1.0, False
   215	
   216

   719	        "# Phase 5a audit-real-run fixture — AUTO-GENERATED, DO NOT HAND-EDIT.\n"

     1	"""核心编排器：从 Notion 读取任务 → 执行 → 对比 → 记录 → 回写"""
     2	
     3	from __future__ import annotations
     4	
     5	import logging
     6	import os
     7	from dataclasses import dataclass
     8	from pathlib import Path
     9	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, Callable, Dict, List, Optional
    10	
    11	from .foam_agent_adapter import FoamAgentExecutor, MockExecutor
    12	from .knowledge_db import KnowledgeDB
    13	from .models import (
    14	    AttributionReport,
    15	    BatchResult,
    16	    CFDExecutor,
    17	    ComparisonResult,
    18	    Compressibility,
    19	    CorrectionSpec,
    20	    ExecutionResult,
    21	    FlowType,
    22	    GeometryType,
    23	    SteadyState,
    24	    SystematicPattern,
    25	    TaskSpec,
    26	)
    27	from .notion_client import NotionClient
    28	from .result_comparator import ResultComparator
    29	from .correction_recorder import CorrectionRecorder
    30	from .error_attributor import ErrorAttributor
    31	
    32	if TYPE_CHECKING:
    33	    from .convergence_attestor import AttestationResult
    34	
    35	PostExecuteHook = Callable[
    36	    [TaskSpec, ExecutionResult, Optional[ComparisonResult], Optional[CorrectionSpec]],
    37	    Any,
    38	]
    39	
    40	CORRECTION_POLICIES = ("legacy_auto_save", "suggest_only")
    41	
    42	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    43	
    44	
    45	@dataclass
    46	class RunReport:
    47	    """单次任务运行的完整报告"""
    48	    task_spec: TaskSpec
    49	    execution_result: ExecutionResult
    50	    comparison_result: Optional[ComparisonResult]
    51	    correction_spec: Optional[CorrectionSpec]
    52	    summary: str
    53	    attestation: Optional["AttestationResult"] = None
    54	    auto_verify_report: Any = None  # AutoVerifyReport or hook-returned status dict, when hook configured
    55	
    56	
    57	class TaskRunner:
    58	    """核心编排器
    59	
    60	    使用方式：
    61	        runner = TaskRunner(executor=MockExecutor())
    62	        reports = runner.run_all()
    63	    """
    64	
    65	    def __init__(
    66	        self,
    67	        executor: Optional[CFDExecutor] = None,
    68	        notion_client: Optional[NotionClient] = None,
    69	        knowledge_db: Optional[KnowledgeDB] = None,
    70	        deviation_threshold: float = 0.10,
    71	        post_execute_hook: Optional[PostExecuteHook] = None,
    72	        correction_policy: str = "legacy_auto_save",
    73	    ) -> None:
    74	        if correction_policy not in CORRECTION_POLICIES:
    75	            raise ValueError(
    76	                f"correction_policy must be one of {CORRECTION_POLICIES}, got {correction_policy!r}"
    77	            )
    78	        # Precedence: explicit executor kwarg > EXECUTOR_MODE env var > MockExecutor
    79	        if executor is not None:
    80	            self._executor: CFDExecutor = executor
    81	        else:
    82	            mode = os.environ.get("EXECUTOR_MODE", "mock").lower()
    83	            if mode == "mock":
    84	                self._executor = MockExecutor()
    85	            elif mode == "foam_agent":
    86	                self._executor = FoamAgentExecutor()
    87	            else:
    88	                raise ValueError(
    89	                    f'EXECUTOR_MODE must be "mock" or "foam_agent", got "{mode}"'
    90	                )
    91	        self._notion = notion_client or NotionClient()
    92	        self._db = knowledge_db or KnowledgeDB()
    93	        self._comparator = ResultComparator(threshold=deviation_threshold)
    94	        self._recorder = CorrectionRecorder()
    95	        self._attributor = ErrorAttributor(knowledge_db=self._db)
    96	        self._post_execute_hook = post_execute_hook
    97	        self._correction_policy = correction_policy
    98	
    99	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   100	    # 公开接口
   101	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   102	
   103	    def run_task(self, task_spec: TaskSpec) -> RunReport:
   104	        """执行单个任务，返回完整报告"""
   105	        logger.info("Running task: %s", task_spec.name)
   106	
   107	        # 1. 执行 CFD
   108	        exec_result = self._executor.execute(task_spec)
   109	        logger.info("Execution success=%s is_mock=%s", exec_result.success, exec_result.is_mock)
   110	
   111	        # 2. 先做收敛 attestation；ATTEST_FAIL 不再进入 compare/correction。
   112	        attestation = self._compute_attestation(exec_result, task_spec)
   113	
   114	        # 3. 加载 Gold Standard
   115	        gold = self._db.load_gold_standard(task_spec.name)
   116	        comparison: Optional[ComparisonResult] = None
   117	        correction: Optional[CorrectionSpec] = None
   118	
   119	        # 4. 对比结果（仅 ATTEST_FAIL short-circuit；HAZARD 仍保留诊断值）
   120	        if (
   121	            gold is not None
   122	            and exec_result.success
   123	            and attestation.overall != "ATTEST_FAIL"
   124	        ):
   125	            comparison = self._comparator.compare(exec_result, gold)
   126	            logger.info("Comparison passed=%s", comparison.passed)
   127	
   128	            # 5. 如有偏差 → 生成 CorrectionSpec (saved only under legacy_auto_save policy)
   129	            if not comparison.passed:
   130	                correction = self._recorder.record(task_spec, exec_result, comparison)
   131	                if self._correction_policy == "legacy_auto_save":
   132	                    self._db.save_correction(correction)
   133	                else:
   134	                    logger.info(
   135	                        "correction_policy=%s: CorrectionSpec built but not persisted",
   136	                        self._correction_policy,
   137	                    )
   138	
   139	        # 6. AutoVerifier post-execute hook (SPEC §INT-1, additive)
   140	        auto_verify_report: Any = None
   141	        if self._post_execute_hook is not None:
   142	            try:
   143	                auto_verify_report = self._post_execute_hook(
   144	                    task_spec, exec_result, comparison, correction
   145	                )
   146	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - hook is optional, must not kill run
   147	                logger.exception("post_execute_hook raised; continuing without verify report")
   148	
   149	        # 7. 生成摘要
   150	        summary = self._build_summary(exec_result, comparison, correction, attestation)
   151	
   152	        # 8. 回写 Notion（Notion 未配置时静默跳过）
   153	        try:
   154	            self._notion.write_execution_result(task_spec, exec_result, summary)
   155	        except NotImplementedError:
   156	            logger.debug("Notion not configured, skipping write-back")
   157	
   158	        return RunReport(
   159	            task_spec=task_spec,
   160	            execution_result=exec_result,
   161	            comparison_result=comparison,
   162	            correction_spec=correction,
   163	            attestation=attestation,
   164	            summary=summary,
   165	            auto_verify_report=auto_verify_report,
   166	        )
   167	
   168	    def run_all(self) -> List[RunReport]:
   169	        """从 Notion 读取所有 Ready 任务并逐一运行"""
   170	        try:
   171	            tasks = self._notion.list_pending_tasks()
   172	        except NotImplementedError:
   173	            logger.warning("Notion not configured; run_all() returns empty list")
   174	            return []
   175	
   176	        reports = []
   177	        for task in tasks:
   178	            report = self.run_task(task)
   179	            reports.append(report)
   180	        return reports
   181	
   182	    def run_batch(self, case_ids: List[str]) -> BatchResult:
   183	        """批量执行指定 case_id 列表（串行，一个失败不阻塞其他）。
   184	
   185	        每个 case 执行 run_task -> compare -> attribute 完整链路。
   186	        """
   187	        results: List[ComparisonResult] = []
   188	        attribution_reports: List[Optional[AttributionReport]] = []
   189	        errors: List[str] = []
   190	        passed = 0
   191	        failed = 0
   192	        total = len(case_ids)
   193	
   194	        for idx, case_id in enumerate(case_ids, 1):
   195	            try:
   196	                task_spec = self._task_spec_from_case_id(case_id)
   197	                report = self.run_task(task_spec)
   198	
   199	                comparison = report.comparison_result
   200	                if comparison is None:
   201	                    comparison = self._ensure_batch_comparison(case_id, report)
   202	
   203	                results.append(comparison)
   204	
   205	                # 归因（即使 passed=True 也做归因）
   206	                if comparison is not None:
   207	                    attribution = self._attributor.attribute(task_spec, report.execution_result, comparison)
   208	                else:
   209	                    attribution = None
   210	                attribution_reports.append(attribution)
   211	
   212	                if report.comparison_result is not None and report.comparison_result.passed:
   213	                    passed += 1
   214	                    print(f"Case {idx}/{total}: {case_id} [PASSED]")
   215	                else:
   216	                    failed += 1
   217	                    print(f"Case {idx}/{total}: {case_id} [FAILED]")
   218	
   219	            except Exception:
   220	                failed += 1
   221	                errors.append(case_id)
   222	                results.append(ComparisonResult(passed=False, summary=f"Exception during {case_id}"))
   223	                attribution_reports.append(None)
   224	                logger.exception("Batch case failed: %s", case_id)
   225	                print(f"Case {idx}/{total}: {case_id} [ERROR]")
   226	
   227	        # Batch-level systematic pattern analysis
   228	        systematic_patterns = self._analyze_systematic_patterns(case_ids, results, attribution_reports)
   229	
   230	        return BatchResult(
   231	            total=total,
   232	            passed=passed,
   233	            failed=failed,
   234	            errors=errors,
   235	            results=results,
   236	            attribution_reports=attribution_reports,
   237	            systematic_patterns=systematic_patterns,
   238	        )
   239	
   240	    def _task_spec_from_case_id(self, case_id: str) -> TaskSpec:
   241	        """从 knowledge_db 通过 case_id 还原 TaskSpec。"""
   242	        chain = self._db.get_execution_chain(case_id)
   243	        if chain is None:
   244	            raise ValueError(f"Unknown case_id: {case_id}")
   245	        parameters = chain.get("parameters", {})
   246	        return TaskSpec(
   247	            name=chain.get("case_name", case_id),
   248	            geometry_type=GeometryType(chain.get("geometry_type", "SIMPLE_GRID")),
   249	            flow_type=FlowType(chain.get("flow_type", "INTERNAL")),
   250	            steady_state=SteadyState(chain.get("steady_state", "STEADY")),
   251	            compressibility=Compressibility(chain.get("compressibility", "INCOMPRESSIBLE")),
   252	            Re=parameters.get("Re"),
   253	            Ra=parameters.get("Ra"),
   254	            Re_tau=parameters.get("Re_tau"),
   255	            Ma=parameters.get("Ma"),
   256	            boundary_conditions={**chain.get("boundary_conditions", {}), **parameters},  # includes aspect_ratio, plate_length, etc.
   257	            description=chain.get("reference", ""),
   258	        )
   259	
   260	    def _ensure_batch_comparison(self, case_id: str, report: RunReport) -> ComparisonResult:

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	"""DEC-V61-036b: Hard comparator gates G3/G4/G5 — post-extraction physics gates.
     2	
     3	Trigger FAIL on:
     4	  * G3 VELOCITY_OVERFLOW  — |U|_max > K * U_ref (K=100 default). VTK-based,
     5	    with log-based epsilon proxy fallback (epsilon ~ u^3/L).
     6	  * G4 TURBULENCE_NEGATIVE — k/epsilon/omega min < 0 at last bounding line
     7	    OR max > 1e+10 (sanity overflow).
     8	  * G5 CONTINUITY_DIVERGED — last-iter sum_local > 1e-2 OR |cumulative| > 1.
     9	
    10	Operates on artifacts already written by the audit pipeline:
    11	  * `reports/phase5_fields/{case_id}/{ts}/log.simpleFoam` (or .pimpleFoam,
    12	    .icoFoam, .buoyantSimpleFoam)
    13	  * `reports/phase5_fields/{case_id}/{ts}/VTK/*.vtk` (latest time step)
    14	
    15	See the accompanying DEC file for ground-truth evidence from the BFS run
    16	(cumulative=-1434.64, k min=-6.41e+30) and expected gate outcomes.
    17	"""
    18	
    19	from __future__ import annotations
    20	
    21	import math
    22	import re
    23	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    24	from pathlib import Path
    25	from typing import Any, Optional
    26	
    27	
    28	def _exceeds_threshold(value: Optional[float], threshold: float) -> bool:
    29	    """True when value is NaN, ±inf, OR finite-and-above threshold.
    30	
    31	    Codex DEC-036b round-1 feedback: plain `value > threshold` returns False
    32	    for NaN, which would silently pass the worst blowup mode. NaN and +inf
    33	    must fire the gate unconditionally.
    34	    """
    35	    if value is None:
    36	        return False
    37	    if math.isnan(value) or math.isinf(value):
    38	        return True
    39	    return value > threshold
    40	
    41	
    42	def _abs_exceeds_threshold(value: Optional[float], threshold: float) -> bool:
    43	    """|value| > threshold with NaN/Inf guard (same semantics as above)."""
    44	    if value is None:
    45	        return False
    46	    if math.isnan(value) or math.isinf(value):
    47	        return True
    48	    return abs(value) > threshold
    49	
    50	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    51	# Thresholds (tunable via per-case override in future; seeded from Codex
    52	# round-1 physics audit on DEC-V61-036).
    53	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    54	
    55	G3_VELOCITY_RATIO_MAX = 100.0     # |U|_max / U_ref
    56	G3_EPSILON_PROXY_MAX = 1.0e10     # fallback when VTK unavailable
    57	G4_TURBULENCE_MAX_OVERFLOW = 1.0e10  # any k/eps/omega max above this = overflow
    58	G5_SUM_LOCAL_MAX = 1.0e-2         # incompressible steady floor
    59	G5_CUMULATIVE_ABS_MAX = 1.0       # hard divergence floor
    60	
    61	
    62	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    63	# Data model
    64	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    65	
    66	@dataclass
    67	class GateViolation:
    68	    """A single post-extraction gate FAIL.
    69	
    70	    The fixture writer forwards these to audit_concerns[] and the
    71	    validation_report verdict engine hard-FAILs on any violation.
    72	    """
    73	
    74	    gate_id: str          # "G3" | "G4" | "G5"
    75	    concern_type: str     # "VELOCITY_OVERFLOW" | "TURBULENCE_NEGATIVE" | "CONTINUITY_DIVERGED"
    76	    summary: str
    77	    detail: str
    78	    evidence: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
    79	
    80	
    81	@dataclass
    82	class LogStats:
    83	    """Parsed telemetry from an OpenFOAM solver log."""
    84	
    85	    final_continuity_sum_local: Optional[float] = None
    86	    final_continuity_cumulative: Optional[float] = None
    87	    # Per-field (k/epsilon/omega) last-iter bounding stats.
    88	    bounding_last: dict[str, dict[str, float]] = field(default_factory=dict)
    89	    # Fatal errors (FOAM FATAL, floating exception).
    90	    fatal_detected: bool = False
    91	    fatal_lines: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    92	
    93	
    94	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    95	# Log parsing
    96	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    97	
    98	# Codex DEC-036b round-1 feedback: token classes below must also accept
    99	# `nan` / `inf` (case-insensitive). When OpenFOAM's floating-point output
   100	# overflows past double range it prints `nan` or `-inf`, and if the regex
   101	# rejected those tokens, the worst blowup mode would silently bypass the
   102	# gates. Each token class is `[\deE+.\-]+|nan|[+\-]?inf` (case-folded).
   103	_NUM_TOKEN = r"(?:[\deE+.\-]+|[nN][aA][nN]|[+\-]?[iI][nN][fF])"
   104	
   105	_CONTINUITY_RE = re.compile(
   106	    r"time step continuity errors\s*:\s*sum local\s*=\s*(" + _NUM_TOKEN + r")\s*,"
   107	    r"\s*global\s*=\s*" + _NUM_TOKEN + r"\s*,"
   108	    r"\s*cumulative\s*=\s*(" + _NUM_TOKEN + r")"
   109	)
   110	
   111	# Matches "bounding k, min: -1.23 max: 4.56 average: 0.1" — the comma+space
   112	# between min and max varies across OF versions; regex tolerates both.
   113	_BOUNDING_RE = re.compile(
   114	    r"bounding\s+(k|epsilon|omega|nuTilda|nut|nuSgs)\s*,\s*"
   115	    r"min\s*:\s*(" + _NUM_TOKEN + r")\s*,?\s*"
   116	    r"max\s*:\s*(" + _NUM_TOKEN + r")"
   117	)
   118	
   119	
   120	def _parse_foam_number(tok: str) -> Optional[float]:
   121	    """Parse a numeric token that may be `nan`, `inf`, `-inf`, or a
   122	    regular finite float. Returns float (nan/inf allowed — callers compare
   123	    against thresholds and NaN/Inf naturally fail any comparison, which
   124	    is the intended "this value is catastrophically bad" signal)."""
   125	    try:
   126	        return float(tok)
   127	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
   128	        return None
   129	
   130	# Tightened to avoid false-positive on the benign startup line
   131	# `sigFpe : Enabling floating point exception trapping (FOAM_SIGFPE)` which
   132	# announces FPE trapping capability, not an actual exception. The real
   133	# fatal markers are FOAM FATAL (IO )?ERROR + stack-trace frames.
   134	_FATAL_RE = re.compile(
   135	    r"FOAM FATAL (IO )?ERROR|"
   136	    r"#\d+\s+Foam::error::printStack|"
   137	    r"^Floating point exception",
   138	    re.MULTILINE,
   139	)
   140	
   141	
   142	def parse_solver_log(log_path: Path) -> LogStats:
   143	    """Parse continuity + bounding lines + fatal markers from a solver log.
   144	
   145	    Extracts the LAST matching occurrence of each pattern (the end-of-run
   146	    state is what matters for gate decisions). For bounding, keeps
   147	    per-field last-iter min/max.
   148	    """
   149	    stats = LogStats()
   150	    if not log_path.is_file():
   151	        return stats
   152	
   153	    last_continuity: Optional[tuple[float, float]] = None
   154	    last_bounding: dict[str, dict[str, float]] = {}
   155	    fatal_lines: list[str] = []
   156	
   157	    with log_path.open("r", encoding="utf-8", errors="replace") as fh:
   158	        for line in fh:
   159	            m = _CONTINUITY_RE.search(line)
   160	            if m:
   161	                sl = _parse_foam_number(m.group(1))
   162	                cum = _parse_foam_number(m.group(2))
   163	                if sl is not None and cum is not None:
   164	                    last_continuity = (sl, cum)
   165	                continue
   166	            m = _BOUNDING_RE.search(line)
   167	            if m:
   168	                field_name = m.group(1)
   169	                field_min = _parse_foam_number(m.group(2))
   170	                field_max = _parse_foam_number(m.group(3))
   171	                if field_min is not None and field_max is not None:
   172	                    last_bounding[field_name] = {
   173	                        "min": field_min,
   174	                        "max": field_max,
   175	                    }
   176	                continue
   177	            if _FATAL_RE.search(line):
   178	                stats.fatal_detected = True
   179	                if len(fatal_lines) < 5:
   180	                    fatal_lines.append(line.strip()[:240])
   181	
   182	    if last_continuity is not None:
   183	        stats.final_continuity_sum_local = last_continuity[0]
   184	        stats.final_continuity_cumulative = last_continuity[1]
   185	    stats.bounding_last = last_bounding
   186	    stats.fatal_lines = fatal_lines
   187	    return stats
   188	
   189	
   190	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   191	# VTK velocity magnitude
   192	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   193	
   194	def read_final_velocity_max(vtk_dir: Path) -> Optional[float]:
   195	    """Return the max |U| across all cells in the latest internal-field VTK.
   196	
   197	    Uses pyvista when available. Returns None when VTK unavailable,
   198	    unreadable, or pyvista is not installed — caller treats None as
   199	    "skip G3 VTK branch, use log-epsilon proxy instead".
   200	    """
   220	
   221	    latest_internal.sort(key=lambda item: (item[0], item[1]))
   222	    vtk_path = latest_internal[-1][2]
   223	
   224	    try:
   225	        mesh = pv.read(str(vtk_path))
   226	    except Exception:
   227	        return None
   228	
   229	    # Look for a vector field named U or velocity.
   230	    point_fields = set(mesh.point_data.keys()) if hasattr(mesh, "point_data") else set()
   231	    cell_fields = set(mesh.cell_data.keys()) if hasattr(mesh, "cell_data") else set()
   232	    U_array = None
   233	    for field_name in ("U", "velocity", "u"):
   234	        if field_name in point_fields:
   235	            U_array = np.asarray(mesh.point_data[field_name])
   236	            break
   237	        if field_name in cell_fields:
   238	            U_array = np.asarray(mesh.cell_data[field_name])
   239	            break
   240	    if U_array is None or U_array.size == 0:
   241	        return None
   242	    # U is typically (N, 3); compute per-cell magnitude.
   243	    if U_array.ndim == 2 and U_array.shape[1] >= 3:
   244	        mags = np.linalg.norm(U_array[:, :3], axis=1)
   245	    else:
   246	        mags = np.abs(U_array.ravel())
   247	    if mags.size == 0:
   248	        return None
   249	    return float(np.nanmax(mags))
   250	
   251	
   252	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   253	# Individual gate checks
   254	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   255	
   256	def _check_g3_velocity_overflow(
   257	    log_stats: Optional[LogStats],
   258	    vtk_dir: Optional[Path],
   259	    U_ref: float,
   260	) -> list[GateViolation]:
   261	    """G3: |U|_max > K * U_ref OR epsilon max > G3_EPSILON_PROXY_MAX."""
   262	    violations: list[GateViolation] = []
   263	    threshold = G3_VELOCITY_RATIO_MAX * max(U_ref, 1e-6)
   264	
   265	    u_max: Optional[float] = None
   266	    if vtk_dir is not None:
   267	        u_max = read_final_velocity_max(vtk_dir)
   268	
   269	    if u_max is not None and _exceeds_threshold(u_max, threshold):
   270	        violations.append(
   271	            GateViolation(
   272	                gate_id="G3",
   273	                concern_type="VELOCITY_OVERFLOW",
   274	                summary=(
   275	                    f"|U|_max={u_max:.3g} exceeds {G3_VELOCITY_RATIO_MAX:.0f}·U_ref "
   276	                    f"({threshold:.3g})"
   277	                )[:240],
   278	                detail=(
   279	                    f"DEC-V61-036b G3: reading latest-time VTK cell velocity "
   280	                    f"found |U|_max={u_max:.6g}, which is above the "
   281	                    f"{G3_VELOCITY_RATIO_MAX:.0f}·U_ref sanity ceiling "
   282	                    f"({threshold:.6g}, U_ref={U_ref}). This indicates "
   283	                    "solver divergence or runaway acceleration — the measurement "
   284	                    "cannot be trusted regardless of whether it lies within "
   285	                    "the gold tolerance band."
   286	                )[:2000],
   287	                evidence={"u_max": u_max, "U_ref": U_ref, "threshold": threshold},
   288	            )
   289	        )
   290	        return violations
   291	
   292	    # Log-epsilon proxy fallback when VTK unavailable.
   293	    if log_stats is not None:
   294	        eps_bound = log_stats.bounding_last.get("epsilon")
   295	        if eps_bound is not None:
   296	            eps_max = eps_bound.get("max")
   297	            if _exceeds_threshold(eps_max, G3_EPSILON_PROXY_MAX):
   298	                violations.append(
   299	                    GateViolation(
   300	                        gate_id="G3",
   301	                        concern_type="VELOCITY_OVERFLOW",
   302	                        summary=(
   303	                            f"epsilon max={eps_max:.3g} implies "
   304	                            f"|U|~{eps_max**(1/3):.2g} (VTK-proxy)"
   305	                        )[:240],
   306	                        detail=(
   307	                            f"DEC-V61-036b G3 (VTK-unavailable fallback): "
   308	                            f"log shows final epsilon max={eps_max:.6g}, "
   309	                            f"above proxy threshold {G3_EPSILON_PROXY_MAX:.0g}. "
   310	                            "Since ε~u³/L, this implies |U| is catastrophically "
   311	                            "large. Velocity overflow flagged from log."
   312	                        )[:2000],
   313	                        evidence={
   314	                            "epsilon_max": eps_max,
   315	                            "proxy_threshold": G3_EPSILON_PROXY_MAX,
   316	                            "inferred_u": eps_max ** (1.0 / 3.0),
   317	                        },
   318	                    )
   319	                )
   320	    return violations
   321	
   322	
   323	def _check_g4_turbulence_negativity(
   324	    log_stats: Optional[LogStats],
   325	) -> list[GateViolation]:
   326	    """G4: k/epsilon/omega min < 0 at last bounding line OR max > overflow."""
   327	    violations: list[GateViolation] = []
   328	    if log_stats is None:
   329	        return violations
   330

     2	#
     3	# Referenced by: src/convergence_attestor.py (loader added per DEC-V61-045 Track 1)

     6	#
     7	# Tier semantics:
     8	# - A1 (solver_exit_clean):   always FAIL
     9	# - A2 (continuity_floor):    HAZARD default; per-case promote_to_fail list can raise
    10	# - A3 (residual_floor):      HAZARD default; per-field + per-case thresholds
    11	# - A4 (iteration_cap):       always FAIL
    12	# - A5 (bounding_recurrence): HAZARD default
    13	# - A6 (no_progress):         HAZARD default; per-case decade-window override
    14	#
    15	# Schema version: 1 (additive-only changes; bump on breaking change)
    16	
    17	schema_version: 1
    18	
    19	defaults:
    20	  # A2: global sum_local floor (incompressible steady target)
    21	  continuity_floor: 1.0e-4
    22	
    23	  # A3: fallback residual floor if field not in residual_floor_per_field
    24	  residual_floor: 1.0e-3
    25	
    26	  # A3: per-field targets (CA-005 fix — field-aware thresholding)
    27	  # Unknown fields fall back to residual_floor above.
    28	  residual_floor_per_field:

    30	    Uy: 1.0e-3
    31	    Uz: 1.0e-3
    32	    p: 1.0e-3
    33	    p_rgh: 1.0e-2     # buoyant/multiphase pressure plateaus one decade higher
    34	    k: 1.0e-3
    35	    epsilon: 1.0e-3
    36	    omega: 1.0e-3
    37	    nuTilda: 1.0e-3
    38	    h: 1.0e-3         # buoyant enthalpy
    39	    T: 1.0e-3
    40	
    41	  # A4: consecutive-capped-block count before FAIL
    42	  iteration_cap_detector_count: 3
    43	
    44	  # A5: bounding-recurrence window + trigger fraction
    45	  bounding_recurrence_frac_threshold: 0.30
    46	  bounding_recurrence_window: 50
    47	
    48	  # A6: no-progress window (outer-step residuals) + min decade-of-decay required
    49	  # CA-006 fix: criterion is <= 1.0 decade (not < 1.0) for "stuck"

    51	  no_progress_window: 50
    52	
    53	# Per-case overrides. Missing keys inherit from defaults.

    55	per_case:
    56

    73	
    74	  circular_cylinder_wake:

    86	  # Steady canonical cases inherit all defaults (no override needed):

   307	                elif freq > 0.5:
   308	                    confidence = "medium"
   309	                else:

   314	                    "sample_config_mismatch": "Review sampleDict configuration — field names may not match generated output",
   315	                    "mesh": "Consider increasing mesh resolution across affected cases",
   316	                    "turbulence": "Review turbulence model selection — kEpsilon may be more stable than kOmegaSST for these geometries",
   317	                    "boundary_condition": "Verify BC setup against reference literature",
   318	                    "solver": "Check solver convergence settings — may need adjusted relaxation or time step",
   319	                }
   320	                patterns.append(SystematicPattern(
   321	                    cause=cause,
   322	                    affected_cases=affected,

   328	
   329	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   330	    # 内部辅助
   331	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   332	
   333	    @staticmethod
   334	    def _build_summary(
   335	        exec_result: ExecutionResult,
   336	        comparison: Optional[ComparisonResult],
   337	        correction: Optional[CorrectionSpec],
   338	        attestation: Optional["AttestationResult"] = None,
   339	    ) -> str:
   340	        parts = []
   341	        status = "✅ Success" if exec_result.success else "❌ Failed"
   342	        parts.append(f"{status} (mock={exec_result.is_mock}, t={exec_result.execution_time_s:.2f}s)")
   343	        if attestation is not None:
   344	            parts.append(f"Attestation: {attestation.overall}")
   345	        if comparison is not None:
   346	            parts.append(f"Comparison: {'PASS' if comparison.passed else 'FAIL'}")
   347	            if comparison.deviations:
   348	                parts.append(f"Deviations: {len(comparison.deviations)}")
   349	        if correction is not None:
   350	            parts.append(f"CorrectionSpec generated: {correction.error_type.value}")
   351	        return " | ".join(parts)
   352	
   353	    def _compute_attestation(
   354	        self,
   355	        exec_result: ExecutionResult,
   356	        task_spec: TaskSpec,
   357	    ) -> "AttestationResult":
   358	        """Run the convergence attestor against the best-available solver log."""
   359	        from .convergence_attestor import AttestationResult, attest
   360	
   361	        log_path = self._resolve_log_path(exec_result)
   362	        case_id = self._resolve_attestation_case_id(task_spec)
   363	        try:
   364	            return attest(
   365	                log_path=log_path,
   366	                execution_result=exec_result,
   367	                case_id=case_id,
   368	            )
   369	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - attestor failure must not kill the task
   370	            logger.exception("Attestation failed; returning ATTEST_NOT_APPLICABLE")
   371	            return AttestationResult(overall="ATTEST_NOT_APPLICABLE", checks=[])
   372	
   373	    def _resolve_attestation_case_id(self, task_spec: TaskSpec) -> str:
   374	        chain = self._db.get_execution_chain(task_spec.name)
   375	        if chain is not None and chain.get("case_id"):
   376	            return str(chain["case_id"])
   377	        return task_spec.name
   378	
   379	    @staticmethod
   380	    def _resolve_log_path(exec_result: ExecutionResult) -> Optional[Path]:
   381	        """Resolve the newest solver log under raw_output_path."""
   382	        if not exec_result.raw_output_path:
   383	            return None
   384	        base = Path(exec_result.raw_output_path)
   385	        if base.is_file():
   386	            return base if base.name.startswith("log.") else None
   387	        if not base.is_dir():
   388	            return None
   389	        log_files = sorted(base.glob("log.*"))
   390	        return log_files[-1] if log_files else None

     1	"""Phase 0 acceptance tests: 3 real cases render a Validation Report.
     2	
     3	Fixtures live in ui/backend/tests/fixtures/{case_id}_measurement.yaml
     4	and are committed alongside the backend. The authoritative
     5	knowledge/gold_standards/*.yaml files are read read-only.
     6	"""

    59	def _sample_thresholds(*, promote_to_fail: frozenset[str]) -> Thresholds:

    73	
    74	def test_hazard_tier_continuity_not_converged_forces_hazard() -> None:
    75	    status, deviation_pct, within_tolerance, _, _ = _derive_contract_status(
    76	        _sample_gold_reference(),
    77	        _sample_measurement(1.02),
    78	        _sample_preconditions(),
    79	        [
    80	            AuditConcern(
    81	                concern_type="CONTINUITY_NOT_CONVERGED",
    82	                summary="final sum_local=5e-4 > floor 1e-4",
    83	            )
    84	        ],
    85	    )
    86	
    87	    assert status == "HAZARD"
    88	    assert within_tolerance is None
    89	    assert deviation_pct == pytest.approx(2.0)
    90	
    91

    95	        _sample_measurement(1.02),
    96	        _sample_preconditions(),
    97	        [

   101	            )

   106	    assert within_tolerance is None

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba ui/backend/tests/test_convergence_attestor.py | sed -n '1,340p'" in /Users/Zhuanz/Desktop/cfd-harness-unified
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	"""DEC-V61-038 tests: convergence attestor A1..A6.
     2	
     3	Coverage: each check's PASS/HAZARD/FAIL branch + LDC/BFS integration
     4	(real audit logs at reports/phase5_fields/*).
     5	
     6	Ground truth from Codex round-1 physics audit (DEC-036):
     7	  LDC    → ATTEST_PASS (all 6 checks PASS or N/A)
     8	  BFS    → ATTEST_FAIL via A2 (sum_local=5.25e+18) + A3 HAZARD + A5 HAZARD
     9	  DHC    → ATTEST_PASS (converged, Nu off gold but physics OK)
    10	"""
    11	
    12	from __future__ import annotations
    13	
    14	from pathlib import Path
    15	from types import SimpleNamespace
    16	
    17	import pytest
    18	
    19	from src import convergence_attestor as ca
    20	from src.convergence_attestor import Thresholds, load_thresholds
    21	
    22	
    23	def _write_log(tmp_path: Path, content: str) -> Path:
    24	    p = tmp_path / "log.simpleFoam"
    25	    p.write_text(content, encoding="utf-8")
    26	    return p
    27	
    28	
    29	def _write_outer_iteration_log(
    30	    tmp_path: Path,
    31	    outer_values: list[float],
    32	    *,
    33	    field: str = "Ux",
    34	    solver: str = "smoothSolver",
    35	    inner_values_per_block: list[list[float]] | None = None,
    36	) -> Path:
    37	    lines: list[str] = []
    38	    for idx, outer_value in enumerate(outer_values, start=1):
    39	        lines.append(f"Time = {idx}s")
    40	        block_values = (
    41	            inner_values_per_block[idx - 1]
    42	            if inner_values_per_block is not None
    43	            else [outer_value]
    44	        )
    45	        for residual in block_values:
    46	            lines.append(
    47	                f"{solver}:  Solving for {field}, Initial residual = {residual}, "
    48	                "Final residual = 0.1, No Iterations 2"
    49	            )
    50	    return _write_log(tmp_path, "\n".join(lines) + "\n")
    51	
    52	
    53	def _make_audit_fixture_report(tmp_path: Path, log_content: str) -> SimpleNamespace:
    54	    from src.models import ComparisonResult, DeviationDetail
    55	
    56	    log = _write_log(tmp_path, log_content)
    57	    execution_result = SimpleNamespace(
    58	        success=True,
    59	        exit_code=0,
    60	        raw_output_path=str(tmp_path),
    61	        key_quantities={"u_centerline": [-0.0415]},
    62	    )
    63	    comparison_result = ComparisonResult(
    64	        passed=True,
    65	        deviations=[
    66	            DeviationDetail(
    67	                quantity="u_centerline[y=0.0625]",
    68	                expected=-0.04192,
    69	                actual=-0.0415,
    70	                relative_error=0.0100,
    71	                tolerance=0.05,
    72	            )
    73	        ],
    74	        summary="within tolerance",
    75	    )
    76	    return SimpleNamespace(
    77	        comparison_result=comparison_result,
    78	        execution_result=execution_result,
    79	        task_spec=None,
    80	        attestation=ca.attest(
    81	            log,
    82	            execution_result=execution_result,
    83	            case_id="lid_driven_cavity",
    84	        ),
    85	    )
    86	
    87	
    88	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    89	# A1 solver_exit_clean
    90	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    91	
    92	def test_a1_passes_on_clean_log(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    93	    log = _write_log(tmp_path, "Time = 1\nExecutionTime = 1 s\nEnd\n")
    94	    result = ca.attest(log)
    95	    a1 = next(c for c in result.checks if c.check_id == "A1")
    96	    assert a1.verdict == "PASS"
    97	
    98	
    99	def test_a1_fails_on_foam_fatal(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
   100	    content = "Time = 1\nFOAM FATAL IO ERROR: missing dict\nExiting\n"
   101	    log = _write_log(tmp_path, content)
   102	    result = ca.attest(log)
   103	    a1 = next(c for c in result.checks if c.check_id == "A1")
   104	    assert a1.verdict == "FAIL"
   105	    assert result.overall == "ATTEST_FAIL"
   106	
   107	
   108	def test_a1_ignores_sigfpe_startup_banner(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
   109	    """DEC-036b Codex nit: 'floating point exception trapping' is a
   110	    startup banner, not an actual exception. Must NOT fire A1."""
   111	    content = (
   112	        "sigFpe : Enabling floating point exception trapping (FOAM_SIGFPE).\n"
   113	        "Time = 1\nEnd\n"
   114	    )
   115	    log = _write_log(tmp_path, content)
   116	    result = ca.attest(log)
   117	    a1 = next(c for c in result.checks if c.check_id == "A1")
   118	    assert a1.verdict == "PASS"
   119	
   120	
   121	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   122	# A2 continuity_floor
   123	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   124	
   125	def test_a2_passes_on_clean_continuity(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
   126	    content = (
   127	        "time step continuity errors : "
   128	        "sum local = 1e-07, global = 1e-09, cumulative = 1e-12\n"
   129	    )
   130	    log = _write_log(tmp_path, content)
   131	    result = ca.attest(log)
   132	    a2 = next(c for c in result.checks if c.check_id == "A2")
   133	    assert a2.verdict == "PASS"
   134	
   135	
   136	def test_a2_hazard_between_floors(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
   137	    """sum_local between A2 floor (1e-4) and G5 floor (1e-2) → HAZARD."""
   138	    content = (
   139	        "time step continuity errors : "
   140	        "sum local = 1e-03, global = 1e-05, cumulative = 0.001\n"
   141	    )
   142	    log = _write_log(tmp_path, content)
   143	    result = ca.attest(log)
   144	    a2 = next(c for c in result.checks if c.check_id == "A2")
   145	    assert a2.verdict == "HAZARD"
   146	
   147	
   148	def test_a2_hazard_above_g5_floor_after_split_brain_fix(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
   149	    """Codex DEC-038 round-1 A2/G5 split-brain fix: A2 no longer returns
   150	    FAIL even for sum_local > 1e-2. That FAIL call belongs to G5 at the
   151	    gate layer. A2 stays strictly HAZARD-tier."""
   152	    content = (
   153	        "time step continuity errors : "
   154	        "sum local = 0.5, global = 0.01, cumulative = 0.1\n"
   155	    )
   156	    log = _write_log(tmp_path, content)
   157	    result = ca.attest(log)
   158	    a2 = next(c for c in result.checks if c.check_id == "A2")
   159	    assert a2.verdict == "HAZARD"  # was FAIL pre-fix
   160

   189	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

   192	
   193	def test_a4_fails_on_consecutive_cap_hits(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
   194	    """5 consecutive Time= blocks each with a capped GAMG p solve → FAIL.
   195	
   196	    Codex round-1 BLOCKER 2: measurement unit changed from consecutive
   197	    lines to consecutive TIME STEPS. Each `Time =` divider opens a new
   198	    block, so this test now needs Time= dividers.
   199	    """
   200	    content = "".join(
   201	        f"Time = {i}\nGAMG:  Solving for p, Initial residual = 0.9, "
   202	        "Final residual = 0.5, No Iterations 1000\n"
   203	        for i in range(5)
   204	    )
   205	    log = _write_log(tmp_path, content)
   206	    result = ca.attest(log)
   207	    a4 = next(c for c in result.checks if c.check_id == "A4")
   208	    assert a4.verdict == "FAIL"
   209	    assert a4.evidence["consecutive_cap_blocks"] >= 3
   210	
   211	
   212	def test_a4_fails_on_p_rgh_buoyant_log(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
   213	    """Codex DEC-038 round-1 BLOCKER 1: impinging_jet stuck solver is
   214	    `GAMG: Solving for p_rgh` in log.buoyantFoam — A4 regex must match
   215	    p_rgh (not just `p,`) to catch the real impinging_jet case.
   216	    """
   217	    content = "\n".join(
   218	        [f"Time = {i}s\nGAMG:  Solving for p_rgh, Initial residual = 0.7, "
   219	         "Final residual = 0.5, No Iterations 1000"
   220	         for i in range(5)]
   221	    )
   222	    log = _write_log(tmp_path, content)
   223	    result = ca.attest(log)
   224	    a4 = next(c for c in result.checks if c.check_id == "A4")
   225	    assert a4.verdict == "FAIL", f"got {a4.verdict}: {a4.summary}"
   226	
   227	
   228	def test_a4_fails_on_dicpcg_p_rgh(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
   229	    """DHC uses DICPCG: Solving for p_rgh. Same regex coverage requirement."""
   230	    content = "\n".join(
   231	        [f"Time = {i*0.5}s\nDICPCG:  Solving for p_rgh, Initial residual = 0.8, "
   232	         "Final residual = 0.6, No Iterations 1000"
   233	         for i in range(1, 6)]
   234	    )
   235	    log = _write_log(tmp_path, content)
   236	    result = ca.attest(log)
   237	    a4 = next(c for c in result.checks if c.check_id == "A4")
   238	    assert a4.verdict == "FAIL"
   239	
   240	
   241	def test_a4_multi_corrector_pimple_counts_blocks_not_lines(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
   242	    """Codex DEC-038 round-1 BLOCKER 2: PIMPLE emits multiple pressure
   243	    solves per Time= block. A4 must count BLOCKS, not LINES — 2 cap-hits
   244	    within the same block should count as 1 toward consecutive threshold,
   245	    not 2. Here 2 blocks × 2 cap-hits = 4 lines but only 2 blocks, so
   246	    consecutive=2 < 3 → PASS. A 3rd capped block is needed to FAIL.
   247	    """
   248	    # 2 capped blocks — should NOT fire (need 3 consecutive blocks).
   249	    content = (
   250	        "Time = 1s\n"
   251	        "GAMG:  Solving for p_rgh, Initial residual = 0.7, Final residual = 0.5, No Iterations 1000\n"
   252	        "GAMG:  Solving for p_rgh, Initial residual = 0.7, Final residual = 0.5, No Iterations 1000\n"
   253	        "Time = 2s\n"
   254	        "GAMG:  Solving for p_rgh, Initial residual = 0.7, Final residual = 0.5, No Iterations 1000\n"
   255	        "GAMG:  Solving for p_rgh, Initial residual = 0.7, Final residual = 0.5, No Iterations 1000\n"
   256	    )
   257	    log = _write_log(tmp_path, content)
   258	    result = ca.attest(log)
   259	    a4 = next(c for c in result.checks if c.check_id == "A4")
   260	    assert a4.verdict == "PASS", f"2 blocks should not fire A4 (threshold=3); got {a4.verdict}"
   261	
   262	
   263	def test_a4_fires_after_three_consecutive_blocks(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
   264	    """3 consecutive capped blocks → FAIL, regardless of per-block count."""
   265	    content = "".join(
   266	        f"Time = {i}s\n"
   267	        "GAMG:  Solving for p_rgh, Initial residual = 0.7, Final residual = 0.5, No Iterations 1000\n"
   268	        "GAMG:  Solving for p_rgh, Initial residual = 0.7, Final residual = 0.5, No Iterations 1000\n"
   269	        for i in range(1, 4)
   270	    )
   271	    log = _write_log(tmp_path, content)
   272	    result = ca.attest(log)
   273	    a4 = next(c for c in result.checks if c.check_id == "A4")
   274	    assert a4.verdict == "FAIL"
   275	    assert a4.evidence["consecutive_cap_blocks"] == 3
   276	
   277	
   278	def test_attestor_not_applicable_when_log_missing(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
   279	    """Codex DEC-038 round-1 comment C: missing log → ATTEST_NOT_APPLICABLE,
   280	    distinct from ATTEST_PASS. DEC-V61-040 UI tiers will surface this
   281	    explicitly for reference/visual_only runs that have no solver log.
   282	    """

   286	    assert result.overall == "ATTEST_NOT_APPLICABLE"
   287

     9	from src.task_runner import TaskRunner, RunReport
    10	from src.foam_agent_adapter import FoamAgentExecutor, MockExecutor
    11	from src.models import (
